make Chicken Tomatillo Tostadas with Refried Black Beans Cabbage-Pepper Slaw.
Remove from oven stand until cool enough handle.
Using your fingers fork, remove meat from bones shred into large pieces; meat aside, discarding bones.
Preheat broiler.
Place tomatillos rimmed baking sheet.
Place chiles, garlic, onion, poblano peppers another rimmed baking sheet.
Transfer both baking sheets (one time, necessary) broiler broil, turning once, until vegetables charred softened, about minutes.
Transfer poblanos small bowl cover with plastic wrap; steam minutes.
remaining vegetables cool slightly peel garlic.
Uncover poblanos and, using paper towel, remove skins.
Remove seeds stems coarsely chop.
Transfer chopped poblanos blender along with tomatillos, chiles, garlic, onion, cilantro, cumin, lime juice; blend until slightly smooth chunks remain season with salt pepper.
Transfer pureed tomatillo mixture large saucepan reserved chicken; place over medium heat simmer until chicken longer pink completely warmed through.
Season with salt pepper more lime juice, desired.
Keep warm until ready use.

Vinci, Smith, Ranelli, 2015.
Selected Physical Characteristics Playing-Related Musculoskeletal Problems Adolescent String Instrumentalists.
Medical problems performing artists.
(3):
143-151.
PURPOSE: Music research investigated prevalence playing-related musculoskeletal problems adults children, prevalence adolescents been established.
String instrumentalists report high problem rates, though unclear whether rates vary between upper lower strings adolescent instrumentalists.
Further, there limited evidence association between physical characteristics playing problems this group musicians.
METHODS: Seventy-six adolescent string musicians from West Australian Youth Orchestras were surveyed.
Their experience playing problems, both symptoms (PRMS) disorders (PRMD), within last month measurements body mass index, hand span, joint mobility (Beighton scale) were obtained.
Prevalence rates were calculated compared between upper lower string instrumentalists using chi-squared test.
Logistic regression examined association physical measures with playing problems, adjusting confounding factors.
RESULTS: Within last month, 73.5% participants reported experiencing PRMS 26.5% reported experiencing PRMD.
There significant difference between problem rates upper lower string instrumentalists.
After adjusting potential confounders, increasing count hypermobile joints remained significantly associated with problems 1.76, 1.02 3.04, p=0.042).
CONCLUSIONS: This study found playing problems common adolescent string instrumentalists, though rates differ between upper lower string players.
Joint hypermobility associated with playing-related problems adolescent musicians.
Early identification problems this group maturing musicians help prevent disabling disorders maximize performance.
